title: [Bug]: Oracle MySQL SQL builder accepts duplicate column mappings
summary: The Oracle MySQL SQL builder allowed multiple normalized fields to map to the same destination column.
observed: A duplicate mapping could generate SQL with repeated column names.
expected: Column mapping validation must reject duplicate destination columns.
reproduction: Run the focused Oracle MySQL hardening regression test for duplicate column mappings.
impact: Repeated destination columns can corrupt operator expectations and produce database-specific behavior.
delivery_semantics: The fix rejects invalid SQL generation before runtime delivery work starts.
security: Explicit mapping validation keeps data-to-column routing predictable and auditable.
acceptance: Oracle MySQL column mappings reject duplicate destination columns.
The default column mapping remains valid.
tests: Run the focused Oracle MySQL hardening regression test and the Oracle MySQL SQL tests.
documentation: Document that mapped columns must be unique.
closeout: Close only after the release containing the SQL validation fix is published.
